# mieza.meanings — LLM reference (inline)

GPU-accelerated k-means for Clojure. Optimized for datasets streamed from disk
(memory-mapped Arrow/Parquet/CSV via tech.ml.dataset), with optional OpenCL
distance computation (not CUDA).

Coordinates (2025-03 era): `ai.mieza/mieza.meanings {:mvn/version "3.0.14"}` in deps.edn;
Leiningen dependency vector `ai.mieza/mieza.meanings "3.0.14"` unless overridden by
`PROJECT_VERSION`.

## Installation

deps.edn:
```
ai.mieza/mieza.meanings {:mvn/version "3.0.14"}
```

project.clj:
```
[ai.mieza/mieza.meanings "3.0.14"]
```

## Quick start

```clojure
(require '[mieza.meanings.kmeans :refer [k-means k-means-seq]]
         '[mieza.meanings.protocols.savable :refer [save-model]]
         '[mieza.meanings.protocols.classifier :refer [assignments]]
         '[mieza.meanings.records.cluster-result :refer [load-model]])

(def dataset "your_dataset.csv")  ;; or .parquet / .arrow path
(def k 10)

;; Single run → ClusterResult record: {:centroids :cost :configuration}
(def model (k-means dataset k))

;; Multiple runs; keep best by total cost
(def best (apply min-key :cost (take 10 (k-means-seq dataset k))))

;; Persist / load (EDN + embedded centroid rows)
(def path "cluster-model.edn")
(save-model best path)
(def reloaded (load-model path))

(:centroids reloaded)
(:cost reloaded)
(:configuration reloaded)

;; Re-score new data chunks (lazy seq of datasets) using saved centroids
;; (assignments reloaded your-dataset-seq)
```

`k-means` is a multimethod on the type of the first argument: `String` file path
or `clojure.lang.LazySeq` of datasets (written to a temp file in the chosen `:format`).

## Core functions

### k-means
Runs Lloyd iteration to convergence or `:iterations` (default 100).
Options (keyword varargs after `k`) merge into defaults:

- `:format` — persistence format for internal materialization (`:arrow` default in code; also `:arrows`, `:parquet`, `:csv`). See `mieza.meanings.persistence/formats`.
- `:init` — initialization method (default `:afk-mc`). See Initialization keys below.
- `:distance-key` — which distance (default `:emd`). See Distance keys below.
- `:m` — chain length where sampling-based inits need it (`default-options` use 200).
- `:columns` — vector of column names to cluster; defaults to all non-assignment columns from the file.
- `:iterations` — max Lloyd rounds (see `lloyd` in kmeans.clj).

Returns `ClusterResult` with:
- `:centroids` — tech.ml.dataset dataset of centroids plus `:assignments` column (cluster indices)
- `:cost` — objective value from `calculate-objective`
- `:configuration` — map `:k`, `:m`, `:distance-key`, `:init`, `:col-names`

### k-means-seq
`(k-means-seq dataset k & options)` — `repeatedly` lazy seq of independent runs; use with `min-key` or filtering.

### save-model / load-model
- `save-model` (protocol `Savable`) on `ClusterResult` writes EDN; centroids serialized via `ds/rows`.
- `load-model` (in `mieza.meanings.records.cluster-result`) reads file and rebuilds dataset with `ds/->dataset`.

### assignments
Protocol `Classifier` on `ClusterResult` and `KMeansState`: `(assignments this dataset-seq)` returns lazy seq of datasets with updated `:assignments` column (GPU/CPU via distance stack).

Other helpers in `kmeans` NS: `estimate-size`, `column-names`, `calculate-objective`, etc.

## Distance functions (`:distance-key`)

From `mieza.meanings.distances/distance-keys`:
`:euclidean`, `:manhattan`, `:chebyshev`, `:correlation`, `:canberra`, `:emd`,
`:euclidean-sq`, `:discrete`, `:cosine`, `:angular`, `:jensen-shannon`.

Default is `:emd` (see namespace docstring — often you want `:euclidean` for standard k-means).

GPU OpenCL kernels exist for `:emd`, `:euclidean`, `:manhattan`, `:chebyshev`,
`:euclidean-sq` when configuration enables GPU (`KMeansState` carries `:use-gpu`;
distance code checks `(:use-gpu conf)`).

## Initialization (`:init`)

Keys from `mieza.meanings.initializations.core/initialization-keys`:
`:afk-mc`, `:k-mc-squared`, `:naive`, `:k-means-parallel`, `:k-means-++`.
Default in `default-options` is `:afk-mc`. Custom methods: extend `initialize-centroids` multimethod.

## Formats (`:format`)

`mieza.meanings.persistence/formats`: `:parquet`, `:arrow` (mmap stream reader),
`:arrows` (IPC), `:csv`. Temporary files use the format’s suffix.

## Testing / JVM

- Tests: `lein test` (GPU tests want OpenCL).
- Dev profile JVM opts include `--add-modules jdk.incubator.vector`, native access flags, large stack — mirror when debugging.

## Security / reporting

See `SECURITY.md`: supported 3.x; report via GitHub private advisory or security@mieza.ai.

## Docs map

- Human narrative: `doc/intro.md`, `README.md`
- Maintainer/agent context: `AGENTS.md`
- Index for tools: `llms.txt` (this file’s short sibling)
